zh.Merthyr Tudful, Glamorgan, Wales: 英國威爾斯格拉莫爾根莫希爾 德費爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Ieaun ap Gwilym of Merthyr Tudful於1287年出生在英國威爾斯格拉莫爾根莫希爾 德費爾時，他的父親Gwilym Sais ap Madog35歲，母親Mrs. Gwilym Sais ap Madog31歲。他至少有1個兒子。他去世於1343年在威爾士享年56歲。

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: William
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When Ieaun ap Gwilym of Merthyr Tudful was born in 1287, in Merthyr Tydfil, Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom, his father, Gwilym Sais ap Madog, was 35 and his mother, Mrs. Gwilym Sais ap Madog, was 31. He had at least 1 son. He died in 1343, in Wales, at the age of 56.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ieaun ap Gwilym of Merthyr Tudful est né en 1287, à Merthyr Tydfil, Glamorgan, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, Gwilym Sais ap Madog, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Gwilym Sais ap Madog, avait 31 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1343, à Galles, à l’âge de 56 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Ieaun ap Gwilym of Merthyr Tudful(이)가 1287년 영국 웨일스 글램르건 머스이르 타이드피에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gwilym Sais ap Madog(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. Gwilym Sais ap Madog(은)는 31세였다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1343년 웨일스에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.
